growth_records.cite: Diao et al. AI-powered high-throughput digital colony picker platform for sorting microbial strains by multi-modal phenotypes. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63929-7. [PMC12514280]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1, Phenotypic characterization of sorted strains under potassium lactate stress: strain S-3-1, Growth rate 0.064.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate directly reported in Table 1 for strain S-3-1 cultured in RMG5 medium (50 g/L glucose, 10 g/L yeast extract, 2 g/L KH2PO4) supplemented with 30 g/L potassium lactate at 30C. Fermentation duration/total elapsed time not stated, so no specific substrate-uptake or product-secretion rate (mmol/gDW/h) could be computed from the accompanying titer/consumption values (glucose consumption, lactate titer, ethanol titer given only as endpoint concentrations, not rates).

growth_records.snippet: S-3-3t ... significant reductions in both specific growth rate (0.072 +/- 0.002 vs 0.080 +/- 0.001 h-1; p<0.001) and maximum biomass ... relative to the plasmid-bearing strain S-3-3.
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate directly reported for CRISPRi-plasmid-cured strain S-3-3t under lactate stress (RMG5-30LA, 30C).

growth_records.cite: Diao et al. AI-powered high-throughput digital colony picker platform for sorting microbial strains by multi-modal phenotypes. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63929-7. [PMC12514280]
growth_records.snippet: the engineered strain S-3-4t retained comparable growth characteristics post-plasmid excision, with no significant differences in growth rate (0.153 +/- 0.001 vs 0.152 +/- 0.001 h-1; p>=0.1) ... relative to the plasmid-b
growth_records.notes: Specific growth rate directly reported for CRISPRi-plasmid-cured strain S-3-4t under lactate stress (RMG5-30LA, 30C); comparable value (0.152) also reported for parental plasmid-bearing S-3-4 in same sentence (near-duplicate of the Table 1 S-3-4 value of 0.154, minor replicate variation).

growth_records.medium: ZRMG (Zymomonas Rich Medium Glucose), no hydrolysate (control)
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: small-scale aerobic batch fermentations in the absence (Figure 1A) or presence of 8% (v/v) batch 2 hydrolysate (Figure 1B). In the presence of hydrolysate, specific ethanol productivity was reduced about three-fold (0.10
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for WT+pJS71 in ZRMG rich medium without hydrolysate, small-scale aerobic batch fermentation, 30C, shaking 200 rpm, fermentation started at 0.1 OD600. Interval used for the calculation stated in Methods as 0-15 h (formula: (ethanol_t2-ethanol_t1)/(t2-t1)/avg(OD600_t1,OD600_t2)). No OD600 endpoint values given so growth rate/doubling time could not be calculated.

growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: In the presence of hydrolysate, specific ethanol productivity was reduced about three-fold (0.10 versus 0.27 g/l/h/OD600).
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for WT+pJS71 in ZRMG + 8% v/v batch-2 miscanthus hydrolysate, aerobic batch fermentation, 30C. Methods list this condition's calculation interval as 0-27 h. A separate, later 4-biological-replicate experiment (Figure 6, paired with ZMO1875 overexpression test) reports a different value (0.16 g/L/h/OD600) for the nominally same WT+hydrolysate condition -- recorded as a distinct observation below because it derives from a separate reported measurement set.

growth_records.medium: ZRMG + miscanthus batch hydrolysate (paired comparator for ZMO1875-overexpression fermentation, Figure 6)
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: overexpression of ZMO1875 improved hydrolysate tolerance and increased specific ethanol productivity 2.4-fold (0.38 versus 0.16 g/l/h/OD600)
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for WT+pJS71 (comparator strain) in hydrolysate-containing fermentation used in the detailed 4-biological-replicate ZMO1875 overexpression comparison (Figure 6/Supplementary Figure 16). Methods state the WT+pJS71-in-hydrolysate interval used was 0-27 h; average of four biological replicates.

growth_records.medium: ZRMG + miscanthus batch hydrolysate + 2% arabinose induction
growth_records.cite: Skerker et al. Dissecting a complex chemical stress: chemogenomic profiling of plant hydrolysates. Molecular Systems Biology (2013). DOI:10.1038/msb.2013.30. [PMC3964314]
growth_records.snippet: We found that overexpression of ZMO1875 improved hydrolysate tolerance and increased specific ethanol productivity 2.4-fold (0.38 versus 0.16 g/l/h/OD600)
growth_records.notes: Directly reported specific ethanol productivity for the ZMO1875-overexpression strain (arabinose-induced Pbad-ZMO1875 on plasmid, in ZM4 background) grown with miscanthus batch hydrolysate, aerobic batch fermentation, 30C, average of four biological replicates; calculation interval stated in Methods as 0-18 h.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined liquid minimal medium (NH4+-replete, 15 mM NH4+)

up.method: not numerically reported in this paper; specific glucose uptake rate stated to be LOWER under NH4+-replete than under N2 fixation; numeric MFA input rates sourced externally (J. McKinlay), not tabulated

sec.method: not numerically reported in this paper; specific ethanol production rate stated to be LOWER under NH4+-replete than under N2 fixation; numeric MFA rates sourced externally, not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Martien et al. Metabolic Remodeling during Nitrogen Fixation in Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00987-21. [PMC8594446]
growth_records.snippet: NH4+ upshift conditions were compared to continuous NH4+-replete conditions; doubling times were approximately 3 h during N2 fixation and 2 h under NH4+... grown with 15 mM NH4+ (replete)
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time under NH4+-replete = 2 h. Calculated growth rate mu = ln2/td = 0.693/2.0 = 0.347 h-1 (growth_method=calculated). Temperature 30C (verbatim). pH not measured on this experimental medium; medium pH adjusted to 6-6.5 range at prep, midpoint 6.25 used. Medium composition is the chemically defined minimal medium; experimental replete cultures used 15 mM NH4+ (starter culture used 1 g/L (NH4)2SO4). 13C-tracer variants used 15 g/L glucose with identical growth rates. Glucose uptake and ethanol production reported only qualitatively (lower under NH4+-replete vs N2 fixation); no per-gDW numeric fluxes are given in the paper (numeric MFA rates were supplied externally by J. McKinlay and not tabulated), so rates left null.

growth_records.medium: Chemically defined liquid minimal medium, no NH4+ (N2-fixing)

up.method: not numerically reported; specific glucose consumption stated to be INCREASED during N2 fixation relative to NH4+-replete; numeric MFA input rates sourced externally, not tabulated
N2 is the sole N source under N2-fixing regime; no specific fixation rate reported

sec.method: not numerically reported; specific ethanol production stated to be INCREASED during N2 fixation relative to NH4+-replete; numeric MFA rates sourced externally, not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Martien et al. Metabolic Remodeling during Nitrogen Fixation in Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2021). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00987-21. [PMC8594446]
growth_records.snippet: doubling times were approximately 3 h during N2 fixation and 2 h under NH4+... during N2 fixation, Z. mobilis exhibits increased specific rates of glucose consumption and ethanol production... nitrogen source was N2 gas (>90% N2 in the anae
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time under N2 fixation = 3 h. Calculated growth rate mu = ln2/td = 0.693/3.0 = 0.231 h-1 (growth_method=calculated). Under N2 fixation Z. mobilis grows slower (lower growth rate/yield) but has higher biomass-specific glucose uptake and ethanol production than NH4+-replete; directions reported qualitatively only, no per-gDW numeric fluxes in paper, so rate values left null. In no-NH4+ medium the 1 g/L (NH4)2SO4 was replaced by 0.2 g/L MgSO4; N2 gas is the sole N source. Temperature 30C. pH taken as prep midpoint 6.25 (medium adjusted to pH 6-6.5).

up.method: 13C-glucose isotope-dilution GC-MS; hourly glucose measurement normalized to gDCW

sec.units: nmol_isoprene mmol_glucose^-1 (yield, not a per-time rate)
sec.method: sealed-vial headspace flame ionization spectrometry (FIS), n=4

growth_records.cite: Khana, Daven B. et al. Systematic Analysis of Metabolic Bottlenecks in the Methylerythritol 4-Phosphate (MEP) Pathway of Zymomonas mobilis. mSystems (2023). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00092-23. [PMC10134818]
growth_records.method: reported — growth rates (h^-1) were measured in three biological replicates from hourly OD600 readings, but the numeric values are given only in Supplementary Table S3, which is not present in the fulltext XML; not extractable here.
growth_records.snippet: Background levels of isoprene production (5.6 nmol_isoprene · mmol_glucose^-1) in Z. mobilis overexpressing GFP were consistent with res...
growth_records.notes: Background/control strain (GFP overexpression). Isoprene yield 5.6 ± 0.5 nmol isoprene/mmol glucose (n=4, Table 2) — this is a product-per-substrate yield, NOT a per-gDW/h rate; placed in secretion_rates for visibility with units flagged. Measured glucose consumption rates (mmol_glucose gDCW^-1 h^-1) and growth rates (h^-1) ARE reported by the paper but only in Supplementary Table S3, absent from the fulltext XML, so numeric qS and mu could not be extracted. Media, anaerobic mode, 30°C, and 20 g/L glucose all explicit in Methods. No rate invented.

growth_records.strain: ZM4 (LL1719; ATCC 31821, wild-type native ethanologen benchmark)
growth_records.medium: ZMM-2 minimal medium (supplemented with methionine and lysine)

up.units: g/L consumed over batch (initial 160 g/L minus 31.3 g/L residual; 714 mM)

sec.units: g/L net produced (final 71.6 g/L / 1553±15 mM minus 10 g/L initially added)

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.method: not reported (long lag phase then exponential per Fig 3; no numeric mu)
growth_records.snippet: For Z. mobilis fermentation, the final ethanol titer reached 1553 ± 15 mM (71.6 ± 0.7 g/L), with 174 ± 2 mM (31.3 ± 0.4 g/L) glucose still present at the end of fermentation.
growth_records.notes: Primary detailed fermentation (Fig 3). Glucose 160 g/L = 888 mM initial; residual 31.3 g/L (174±2 mM); consumed = 128.7 g/L (714 mM). Ethanol final 71.6 g/L (1553 mM) but 10 g/L (~217 mM) was added at start, so net produced = 61.6 g/L (1336 mM). Yield = 61.6/128.7 = 0.479 g/g; molar 1336/714 = 1.87 mol/mol = 94% of theoretical 2.0. Z. mobilis uses Entner-Doudoroff pathway. No pyruvate accumulation observed; authors infer titer limited by substrate uptake not central metabolism. No gDW/specific-rate data.

up.units: g/L initial loading (consumption not reported for this row)

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.snippet: Z. mobilis ZM4 ... 67 g/L ZMM 150 g/L glucose 10 g/L [yeast extract] – 37 [°C] This study
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (this study): ZMM with 10 g/L yeast extract, no pH control → 67 g/L ethanol. Compare 8 g/L (ZMM no YE) and 45 g/L (ZMM-2 +Met/Lys no YE) same table. This ZMM row lacks the 2 g/L Met + 2 g/L Lys of ZMM-2. Consumption not reported.

growth_records.medium: ZMM medium (no yeast extract, no Met/Lys)

up.units: g/L initial loading (consumption not reported for this row)

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.snippet: Z. mobilis ZM4 ... 8 g/L ZMM 150 g/L glucose 0 [yeast extract] – 37 [°C] This study
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (this study): plain ZMM without yeast extract and without Met/Lys gave only 8 g/L ethanol (Z. mobilis is auxotrophic for lysine and methionine). Supplementing ZMM-2 with 2 g/L Met + 2 g/L Lys raised titer to 45 g/L (separate record). Consumption not reported.

growth_records.medium: ZMM-2 medium (+2 g/L Met, +2 g/L Lys; no yeast extract, no pH control)

up.units: g/L initial loading (consumption not reported for this row)

growth_records.cite: Sharma, Bishal Dev et al. Metabolic imbalance limits fermentation in microbes engineered for high-titer ethanol production. mSystems (2026). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00074-26. [PMC13098246]
growth_records.snippet: Z. mobilis ... 45 g/L ZMM-2 150 g/L glucose 0 [yeast extract] – [no pH] 37 [°C] This study
growth_records.notes: Table 2 (this study): ZMM-2 (Met/Lys added) without yeast extract, no active pH control → 45 g/L ethanol. Adding pH 6.0 control and raising glucose to 160 g/L (with 10 g/L initial ethanol) gave the 72 g/L detailed Fig 3 record. Initial pH 6.0 set with H2SO4 per Methods. Consumption not reported.

growth_records.medium: Zymomonas minimal media (ZMM), glucose sole carbon source

up.units: consumed (mM over 24 h; absolute rate in mmol/gDW/h not derivable)

yields.product_yields: Ethanol = 92.6% of glucose consumed; using 2 mol ethanol per mol glucose theoretical, yield = 0.926 x 2 = 1.85 mol EtOH/mol glucose. Gluconate <0.01% of glucose. Lactate, shikimate, succinate each <0.1% of glucose.

growth_records.cite: Martien JI et al. Systems-Level Analysis of Oxygen Exposure in Zymomonas mobilis: Implications for Isoprenoid Production. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00284-18. [PMC6372839]
growth_records.snippet: ethanol ... accounted for 92.6% of the glucose consumed for anaerobic cultures ... Anaerobic cultures did not accumulate gluconate to any appreciable extent (<0.01% of glucose was converted to gluconate).
growth_records.notes: ANAEROBIC control condition, Z. mobilis ZM4, 30C, minimal media, glucose sole C source, batch. Growth measured as OD600 (experimental). growth_rate CALCULATED: paper states cultures 'underwent approximately one doubling during the 2-h time course' at mid-exponential => doubling_time approx 2 h; mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/2 = 0.347 h-1 (rounded 0.35). Ethanol yield 92.6% of glucose consumed (Fig 8C, NMR); gluconate negligible (<0.01%). Absolute uptake/secretion in mmol/gDW/h NOT derivable: paper reports OD600, extracellular mM concentrations and %-of-glucose fractions but no gDW biomass conversion nor time-resolved slope for a defined interval. Native ethanologen converting up to 96% glucose to ethanol. This is a targeted-metabolomics + 13C-tracing study, not a quantitative net-flux 13C-MFA (13C used qualitatively to trace label incorporation into MEP-pathway intermediates), so mfa_fluxes empty.

growth_records.medium: Zymomonas minimal media (ZMM), glucose sole carbon source

up.units: consumed (mM over 24 h; absolute rate in mmol/gDW/h not derivable)

sec.units: 11.5 mM final extracellular = 18% of glucose consumed
2 uM extracellular by 30 min (transient overflow of MEP-pathway intermediate)

yields.product_yields: Ethanol = 35% of glucose consumed => 0.35 x 2 = 0.70 mol EtOH/mol glucose. Gluconate = 18% of glucose consumed = 0.18 mol gluconate/mol glucose (1:1 stoichiometry), final 11.5 mM. 42% of consumed glucose unaccounted for by detected metabolites (CO2 not measured).

growth_records.cite: Martien JI et al. Systems-Level Analysis of Oxygen Exposure in Zymomonas mobilis: Implications for Isoprenoid Production. mSystems (2019). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00284-18. [PMC6372839]
growth_records.snippet: The final extracellular gluconate concentration was 11.5 mM, representing 18% of the glucose consumed ... ethanol ... accounted for ... only 35% for aerobic cultures ... 42% of the glucose consumed by aerobically grown cultures did not corr
growth_records.notes: AEROBIC condition (O2 shift), Z. mobilis ZM4, 30C, minimal media, glucose sole C, batch. Oxygen inhibited both growth and ethanol production; growth 'markedly lower' under aerobic than anaerobic but NO numeric aerobic mu/doubling reported for the 24-h course, so growth_rate/doubling left null (growth_method reported/qualitative). Carbon utilization fractions (Fig 8C, NMR + LC-MS): ethanol 35% of glucose consumed => yield 0.35 x 2 = 0.70 mol/mol; gluconate 18% => 0.18 mol/mol, final 11.5 mM; 42% of glucose unaccounted (CO2 not measured). Gluconate excreted continuously over 24 h (redox balancing via glucose->gluconate diverting electrons to ETC). Acetate and pyruvate increased under aerobic (not %-quantified). Extracellular MEcDP transiently 2 uM by 30 min. Absolute rates in mmol/gDW/h NOT derivable (no gDW biomass, only OD600 and mM). 13C used qualitatively to trace label incorporation, not a quantitative net-flux 13C-MFA.

up.method: 13C-MFA constraint; glucose consumption rate measured at exponential phase and used as MFA constraint

sec.method: fermentation production rate measured as MFA constraint (value in Supplementary Table 1, not in retrievable text)

mfa.note: 13C-MFA of ZM4 under aerobic condition shows greater influx to BOTH acetate and acetoin vs anaerobic reference; numeric central-carbon flux map is in Supplementary Fig. 2a and MFA constraints (growth rate, glucose consumption rate, fermentation production rate) in Supplementary Table 1 — not available in main-text XML

growth_records.cite: Xiongying Yan et al. Paradigm of engineering recalcitrant non-model microorganism with dominant metabolic pathway as a biorefinery chassis. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54897-5. [PMC11608335]
growth_records.snippet: Cell growth rate, glucose consumption rate and fermentation production rate were characterized for exponential phase growth at 30 °C in MM medium under 10% medium volume condition for using as MFA constraints (Supplementary Table 1).
growth_records.notes: Genuine experimental 13C-MFA condition of wild-type Z. mobilis ZM4 used to validate GEM eciZM547. [1,2-13C]glucose sole carbon source. Measured growth rate, glucose uptake rate, and product secretion rates EXIST (explicitly stated as measured and used as MFA constraints) but their NUMERIC values reside in Supplementary Table 1 / Supplementary Fig. 2a, which are not present in the main-text XML — hence rate=null. No number was invented. Note: the model max predicted growth rate 0.50 /h and max ethanol production 134.76 mmol/gDW/h are MODEL predictions (eciZM547), NOT experimental, so excluded from rate fields.

growth_records.strain: ZMB6 (recombinant D-lactate producer; 2,3-BDO intermediate-chassis background with Dldh / LmldhA lactate dehydrogenase)

up.method: nearly all glucose consumed (~142 g/L in) converted to D-lactate; per-time and per-gDW rate not reported in main text (Supplementary Table 2)

sec.method: HPLC; final titer in shake-flask batch

growth_records.cite: Xiongying Yan et al. Paradigm of engineering recalcitrant non-model microorganism with dominant metabolic pathway as a biorefinery chassis. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54897-5. [PMC11608335]
growth_records.snippet: Nearly all glucose was converted into D-lactate with a titer of 140.92 g/L and a yield of 99% by the recombinant strain ZMB6 (Fig. 4c, Supplementary Table 2).
growth_records.notes: Experimental fermentation of engineered Z. mobilis ZMB6 on glucose in RM rich medium. Reported as titer (140.92 g/L) and product yield (99% = 0.99 g D-lactate / g glucose). No specific growth rate, doubling time, or per-gDW uptake/secretion rate given in main text (kinetic time-course in Supplementary Table 2, not in XML). RM base composition (10 g/L yeast extract, 2 g/L KH2PO4) taken from Methods (RMG5 definition minus its 50 g/L glucose); glucose was varied to high concentration to reach 140.92 g/L product. Rates are volumetric (g/L), not per-biomass.

up.method: CRH supplied ~107 g/L glucose; consumed for D-lactate; per-gDW/time rate not reported

sec.method: reported volumetric productivity for CRH fermentation

growth_records.cite: Xiongying Yan et al. Paradigm of engineering recalcitrant non-model microorganism with dominant metabolic pathway as a biorefinery chassis. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54897-5. [PMC11608335]
growth_records.snippet: The titer, productivity, and yield of D-lactate of this study are 104.6 g/L, 2.32 g/L/h, and 97%, respectively, which are higher than the values reported in previous studies.
growth_records.notes: Experimental fermentation of engineered Z. mobilis ZMB6 on corncob residue hydrolysate (lignocellulosic feedstock). Reported titer 104.6 g/L, volumetric productivity 2.32 g/L/h, product yield 97% (0.97 g/g). Temperature 33 °C (from Methods: 'D-lactic acid production from CRH ... at 33 °C on rotary shaker 100 rpm, 2 g/L yeast extract supplemented'); Fig. 4d indicates a fermentor run. No growth rate/doubling time reported; rates are volumetric not per-gDW. All numbers verbatim from text; none derived.

growth_records.medium: Minimal defined medium with glucose as sole carbon source (phosphate-buffered, pH 7)

up.method: HPLC (Aminex-87H); uptake rate = slope of [glucose] vs cell density

mfa.note: Fig. 1 FBA flux map for Z. mobilis (homoethanol fermentation); per-product relative fluxes only in figure image; absolute values in SI Appendix Table S6

growth_records.cite: Mueller, Elliott P. et al. Metabolic controls on the carbon isotope fractionations of bacterial fermentation. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2025). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2511040122. [PMC12541407]
growth_records.snippet: Z. mobilis excreted exclusively ethanol ... Z. mobilis had a slower (0.25 h-1) growth rate (SI Appendix, Fig. S1).
growth_records.notes: mu=0.25 h-1 reported (OD600, SI Fig. S1). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.25 = 2.772 h. Homoethanol fermenter; excretes ethanol exclusively (measured by HPLC). Strain not named in main text. Phosphate buffer pH 7. Full medium in SI Tables S1-S5 -> not stated. Absolute glucose uptake/ethanol secretion rates in SI Table S6; rate fields null (not invented). Growth temperature not stated in main text.
